panels.title: Peers Timeline
panels.description: Connected peers (live P2P sessions) vs discovered peers (addresses in the peer store) over time. The gap between the two lines is normal — nodes know many more peers than they connect to.

panels.title: Discovered Peers
panels.description: Total peer addresses stored in each node's peer store (bootnodes + DHT + gossip). This is **not** live connections — a node can know many more peers than it is connected to.

panels.title: Pending Connections
panels.description: P2P handshakes currently in progress per node. Brief spikes are normal; sustained high values may indicate connection churn or network issues.

panels.title: Sync Peers
panels.description: Peers actively used for block synchronization. Distinct from total connected peers — only a subset of connections participate in chain sync.

panels.title: Connection Close Reasons (Last 1h)
panels.description: Why P2P connections closed in the last hour, grouped by reason label. Useful for diagnosing resets, timeouts, and policy-driven disconnects.

panels.title: Distinct Peers Connection Rate
panels.description: Rate of new connections opened/closed to **unique** peer IDs (not duplicate reconnects to the same peer). Measures network churn at the peer level.

panels.title: Avg Notification Size - Incoming
panels.description: Average payload size of incoming gossip notifications by protocol (block announces vs transactions). Large transaction notifications may indicate batch gossip or large extrinsics.

panels.title: Avg Notification Size - Outgoing
panels.description: Average payload size of outgoing gossip notifications by protocol. Useful for spotting unusually large block or transaction propagations.

panels.title: DHT Records Count
panels.description: Number of peer records stored in the Kademlia DHT routing table. Reflects how much of the network topology each node has learned.

panels.title: Kademlia Random Queries Rate
panels.description: Rate of random DHT lookups performed for peer discovery. Low activity is normal on stable networks; drops to zero may indicate DHT issues.

panels.title: K-Buckets Distribution
panels.description: How many peers sit in each Kademlia k-bucket (by bucket index). A healthy node typically has entries spread across multiple buckets.

panels.title: Incoming Request Latency
panels.description: Latency of successful incoming libp2p requests (p50 / p95 / p99). High p95/p99 on block or state requests can slow sync for peers calling this node.

panels.title: Outgoing Request Latency
panels.description: Latency of successful outgoing libp2p requests (p50 / p95 / p99). Rising values may mean slow or overloaded upstream peers.

panels.title: Sync Queue Status
panels.description: Block sync queue depth and download/import pipeline state. Growing queues while the node is not in major sync may indicate the node is falling behind.

panels.title: Connection Error Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of connection attempts that fail (handshake errors, timeouts). Sustained elevation suggests network or firewall problems.

panels.title: Incoming vs Outgoing Connections Ratio
panels.description: Ratio of inbound to outbound P2P connections. Large imbalance may indicate NAT, firewall, or bootnode reachability asymmetry.
